_meta.title: AI Compute Demand Radar — Assumptions
_meta.note_zh: 本檔為『手動、可更新』的假設與外部錨點來源。yfinance 抓不到的數(分析師 TAM、IEA 電力、各廠分部營收、轉換比率)都放這裡，每項附 source / as_of / confidence。要更新預測，改這裡的數字＋日期即可，再按 dashboard 的 Refresh 重算。
_meta.note_en: This file holds the MANUAL, sourced assumptions that yfinance cannot fetch (analyst TAM, IEA power, vendor segment revenue, conversion ratios). Every block carries source / as_of / confidence. To update the forecast, edit numbers + date here, then hit Refresh.

hyperscaler_capex_usd_bn._source: Company FY2025 reports + 2026 guidance; visualcapitalist/valueaddvc/CNBC/Futurum compilations (as_of 2026-02). 2027-2030 = extrapolation (no firm guidance issued that far out).
hyperscaler_capex_usd_bn._confidence: high (2024-25 actuals) / medium (2026 guide) / low (2027-30 extrapolation)
hyperscaler_capex_usd_bn._note: Spine of the top-down model. yfinance live capex (TTM) is shown as context, NOT substituted into the path; see data_sources.py. by_company carries actual/guided years; out-year extrapolation is held separately in extrapolated_total so it is never mistaken for company guidance.

extrapolated_total._note: Out-year totals where no firm company guidance exists. Extrapolated from the 2026 guided sum (~$660B) at a decelerating growth rate (consistent with the global path slowing toward 2030). Flagged 'extrapolated' in the snapshot so the KPI never silently shows 0 for 2027-2030.

global_dc_capex_usd_bn._source: Independent anchor: Dell'Oro / IDC global DC capex prints (colo + enterprise + sovereign + neocloud + hyperscaler). NOT derived from the top-hyperscaler sum.
global_dc_capex_usd_bn._note: INDEPENDENT global anchor path, not a gross-up of the hyperscaler spine. The implied ratio global/(top-hyperscaler sum) is ~1.2-1.9 and drifts year to year, so a single gross-up factor would be misleading — we keep this as its own sourced path. (Earlier versions carried a 1.30 gross_up_factor that the model never used; removed to avoid implying a derivation that does not exist.)

ai_dc_capex_share._source: IEA Energy & AI 2025 (AI = 35-50% of DC power by 2030) + hyperscaler commentary (~75% of 2026 capex AI-tied, Futurum/CNBC)

silicon_conversion_band._source: Calibrated so 2025 mid reconciles with observed merchant vendor revenue (~$200B) and Omdia 2025 TAM ($207B). Represents fraction of AI-DC capex realized as compute accelerator + CPU silicon/system value.
silicon_conversion_band._note: The single biggest swing factor. Shown as a band, not a point, on purpose. CALIBRATION CAVEAT: the mid coefficient is back-solved (reverse-calibrated) to hit the single Omdia 2025 point — it is NOT an independent estimate, so the 2025 top-down-vs-analyst 'agreement' is partly by construction, not a true cross-validation.

category_mix._source: Omdia/Bloomberg Intelligence: GPU dominant 2024 (~78-80% of accelerator $), custom ASIC fastest-growing (43%+ CAGR), share rising toward ~30% by 2030. CPU is the smaller general-compute/head-node slice.
category_mix._note: Share of total AI compute silicon $ by category, must sum ~1.0 per year.

asp_usd._source: Blended ASPs across product mix. GPU = H100/H200/B200/GB200 blend (system-attached, incl HBM). ASIC = TPU/Trainium/MTIA/custom blend. CPU = server CPU blend.
asp_usd._note: Used to convert $ -> unit counts. Mild annual drift applied so unit counts are not a pure mirror of $ (was previously 0 for GPU/ASIC). GPU rises slightly as the blend shifts to rack-scale GB200/system-attached SKUs; ASIC drifts gently down on volume/process maturity; CPU down on commoditization. Units come out in MILLIONS of accelerators/CPUs.

bottomup_vendor_anchors_usd_bn._source: Latest earnings as researched 2026-06: NVDA DC Q4FY26 $62.3B/q (annualized ~$249B); AVGO AI semi Q1FY26 $8.4B +106% YoY, Q2 guide $10.7B (annualized ~$45B); MRVL DC custom XPU run-rate; INTC DCAI; AMD DC (Instinct + EPYC split).
bottomup_vendor_anchors_usd_bn._confidence: high (merchant revenue) / low (internal custom ASIC deployed value)
bottomup_vendor_anchors_usd_bn._note: These are ANNUALIZED run-rates representing calendar ~2026. live_scale: at refresh we scale each anchor by (live_TTM_revenue / anchor_ttm_at_capture). anchor_ttm is each company's TOTAL revenue TTM measured at capture (2026-06-13 via yfinance), so the first refresh is neutral (ratio~1.0) and only a NEW earnings print moves the estimate. Total-company revenue is a freshness proxy for the segment line.
bottomup_vendor_anchors_usd_bn.caliber_note_zh: 口徑:這些是『前瞻年化 run-rate』(最新一季 ×4 或前瞻指引),代表日曆 ~2026,刻意領先官方『已截止財年』申報。故 NVDA 資料中心錨點 $249B(單一分部年化)會高於 SEC EDGAR 全公司年報 $215.9B(截至 FY26 1 月)——前者是前瞻分部 run-rate、後者是已截止全公司財年,兩者口徑不同、不可直接相減。
bottomup_vendor_anchors_usd_bn.caliber_note_en: Caliber: these are FORWARD ANNUALIZED run-rates (latest quarter ×4 or forward guide) representing calendar ~2026, deliberately ahead of official CLOSED-fiscal-year filings. So the NVDA Data Center anchor $249B (single-segment annualized) sits ABOVE the SEC EDGAR full-company FY26 (ended Jan) revenue of $215.9B — the former is a forward segment run-rate, the latter a closed full-company fiscal year; different calibers, not directly subtractable.

nvidia_datacenter._note: Forward annualized DC-segment run-rate (Q4FY26 $62.3B/q ×4). Exceeds full-company closed-FY EDGAR revenue by design — see caliber_note.

internal_custom_asic._note: Google TPU + AWS Trainium/Inferentia + Meta MTIA DEPLOYED value beyond merchant design fees. Not a public revenue line — estimate only.

arm_based_server_cpu._note: Graviton/Axion/Grace + other Arm server CPU deployed value estimate.

analyst_tam_benchmarks_usd_bn._source: Published third-party AI accelerator/AI-chip TAM forecasts (as_of 2025-2026).

scenarios._source: Spread around the base capex path; bull = sustained 75%+ AI capex + slower ASP erosion; bear = capex digestion / overbuild correction.

power_cross_check._source: IEA 'Energy and AI' 2025: global DC electricity 415 TWh (2024, 1.5% of world) -> ~945 TWh (2030, <3%); ~1200 TWh (2035). AI share of DC power 5-15% recent -> 35-50% by 2030.

supply_anchors._source: TSMC earnings 2025: HPC >60% of revenue (Q1'25); CoWoS advanced-packaging capacity ~35k wpm (2024) -> ~70k (end-2025) -> ~150k (2026 exit-rate); tight through 2027. The per-year value here is the year EXIT-rate, so it ramps within the year (~80k mid-2026 / Q2 exit, cf. /bottleneck's tsmc_cowos seed, then ~150k by end-2026 — same ramp, different point). Advanced packaging is the binding supply constraint on AI accelerator units.

workload_split._source: McKinsey/IDC/MarketsandMarkets: inference 33% of AI compute (2023) -> 65%+ (2029) -> ~two-thirds (2030); inference overtakes training mid-decade. AI inference market $106B (2025) -> $255B (2030).
workload_split._note: Share of ACCELERATOR (GPU+ASIC) compute $ by workload. Training-heavy early, inference-dominant by 2030. Applied to gpu+asic only (CPU excluded).

buyer_split._source: IDC AI infrastructure spend $153B(2024)->$318B(2025)->>$1T(2029); sovereign AI a ~$600B market by 2030 (France EUR109B, Saudi $1.44B->$6.4B, S.Korea mega-program); neocloud (CoreWeave/Nebius/Lambda) a distinct buyer.
buyer_split._note: Share of total AI data-center compute silicon $ by BUYER type. Hyperscaler dominant but sovereign + enterprise gain share. Must sum ~1.0.

edge_ai_silicon_usd_bn._source: MarketsandMarkets edge AI hardware $26.1B (2025) -> $58.9B (2030), 17.6% CAGR. SEPARATE end market from data-center silicon (on-device/edge inference: phones, PCs, cars, IoT, robotics).
edge_ai_silicon_usd_bn._note: Additive segment shown ALONGSIDE data-center AI silicon to give a true 'all-of-world' AI compute view. High-volume / low-ASP -> unit count omitted (too imprecise).

edgar_cik_map._source: SEC EDGAR central index keys (10-digit). Used to pull OFFICIAL total revenue + capex from companyfacts XBRL (authoritative, no scraping). Foreign filers (TSM 20-F) excluded.
edgar_cik_map._note: companyfacts returns AGGREGATE facts only — true business-segment (e.g. NVDA Data Center) is NOT in companyfacts; it needs per-filing dimensional XBRL. We therefore scale the sourced segment anchors by EDGAR-official TOTAL revenue (a documented proxy).

fred_macro_series._source: FRED (St. Louis Fed). Demand-environment cross-check: is the macro that funds compute capex expanding? Fetched via no-key fredgraph CSV; FRED_API_KEY used if present.

series.label_zh: 資訊處理設備投資 (年化十億美元)
半導體與電子零件 工業生產指數
